entries.name: The Devil Wears Prada 2
entries.description: This is the version of the world I’d choose to live in for the entire summer, one where the fashion world icon Miranda Priestly reunites with her former assistant, Andy, to take on Emily, her other former assistant, who has become Miranda's main rival. The joke that the legendary Runway editor-in-chief can’t remember either of her one-time PAs? Without flaw. Kudos to the studio for bringing back director David Frankel and screenwriter Aline Brosh McKenna for a sequel that doesn't get lost in layers of tuille and A-list cameos, keeping the dramedy focused on the three women (and  Stanley Tucci) who made the original movie eternally rewatchable. Also I totally will not miss Nate. — Arno

entries.name: Hokum
entries.description: After the first dream-like teaser filled with bug-eyed bunny men and claustrophobic dumbwaiters, I knew Oddity writer-director Damian McCarthy’s latest was going to the top of my Watchlist. Adam Scott stars as an author on a trip to honor his late parents when spooky things start happening at his remote Irish inn. We’re going full folk horror here, folks, and all I can say is sign me up. Expect creepy staring villagers, inscrutable ominous warnings, and surreal hauntings as Scott spirals into the darkest corners of hell. — Adrienne

entries.name: Lord of the Flies
entries.description: I’ve got the conch, and I say it’s high time we had a new adaptation of William Golding’s classic tale of lost boys and their brutal descent into savagery. And I’m not alone. Series creator Jack Thorne agrees, telling Netflix’s Tudum, “As a society, we’re having a conversation right now about boys. We’re losing a generation of boys, and we’re losing it because of the hate they are ingesting — because it is an answer to their loneliness and isolation.” Thorne was one half of the creative duo behind the Emmy- and Golden Globe-winning series “Adolescence,” which explored similar themes in a modern setting, making him the perfect writer to take on this unfortunately timely topic. — Adrienne

entries.name: The Terror: Devil in Silver
entries.detail: Season 3 premieres May 7 on AMC+ and Shudder
entries.description: Although the settings and time periods seem unconnected, one theme runs through this horror anthology series: when trapped with supernatural horrors, the lines between man and monster begin to blur. Season 3 adapts Victor LaValle's 2012 horror novel “Devil in Silver,” following Pepper (Dan Stevens), a working-class moving man who finds himself wrongfully committed to New Hyde Psychiatric Hospital, an institution filled with those society would rather forget and perhaps even the Devil himself.  — Vanessa

entries.name: Mortal Kombat 2
entries.description: Karl Urban joining the ensemble for this follow-up to the 2021 fantasy-action flick is the kind of perfect casting we can get behind. He’s playing Johnny Cage, if that wasn’t obvious already, a past-his-prime fighter who finds himself thrust into a battle to save Earthrealm from the sorcerer Shao Kahn, the tyranical Emperor of Outworld. This is the fourth MK feature film and the second in director Simon McQuoid’s reboot of the franchise. Expect a lot of action and just enough plot to keep things moving as Cage and the crew race to defeat the evil Emperor and, hopefully, finish him. — Adrienne

entries.name: The Sheep Detectives
entries.description: This adaptation of the novel Three Bags Full: A Sheep Detective Story employs the cast of the year to tell the story of a flock of sheep who investigate the murder of their beloved shepherd. Just as impressive as the cast list is the fact that “Chernobyl”/”The Last of Us” creator Craig Mazin returned to his comedy roots to write the screenplay. The movie, which stars Hugh Jackman as the mourned shepherd, serves as an X-Men reunion of sorts: Patrick Stewart lends his voice to Sir Ritchfield, the head ram known for his wandering mind. — Arno

entries.name: The Punisher: One Last Kill
entries.description: I feel Frank Castle is an underrated, morally gray on-screen boyfriend. Truthfully, I don't have a lot to say about this hourlong TV movie other than I’m in love with Castle and enjoy watching him beat the s*** out of bad guys. Don’t think this is the vigilante’s last go-round since we already know he’s a major part of Spider-Man’s brand-new day this summer. — Hillary

entries.name: Good Omens
entries.detail: Series finale premieres on Prime Video May 13
entries.description: This isn’t the way we wanted to say farewell to the Ineffable Husbands Aziraphale and Crowley, but real-world interference, the sexual misconduct allegations against creator Neil Gaiman, caused the final season of “Good Omens” to be trimmed back into a single 90-minute episode. The series finale looks to pick up soon after the heartbreaking events of Season 2, where Aziraphale chose to ascend to the role of Supreme Archangel and reject Crowley’s plea to be together for eternity. The inevitable reunion comes after Heaven loses track of Jesus, who is being hunted by demons on Earth, causing Aziraphale to swoop up Crowley for one last big swing to save humankind. — Arno

entries.name: Welcome to Wrexham
entries.description: The highly addictive “Welcome to Wrexham” often feels more  like a fairy tale than a documentary series, and that's exactly why I've  stayed invested for the past four seasons. No longer the scrappy underdogs  from Season 1, Wrexham A.F.C. has gone from 15 seasons in the fifth-tier  National League to the second-tier EFL Championship in just three consecutive  promotions. (Which is also a record, BTW.) But now that they've made the  leap, can they hold on? With the series renewed through 2029, we'll be  rooting for Rob, Ryan, and The Reds for the foreseeable future. Up the town!  — Vanessa

entries.name: Obsession
entries.description: The powerful simplicity of this film’s premise is matched by how freaky  it’s willing to get while interrogating that central concept: What happens  when a boy’s wish that a girl loved him more than anyone else alive comes  true? The answer is obviously “bad stuff” in writer/director Curry Barker’s  debut horror feature that's been building fan interest at TIFF, Fantastic  Fest, and SXSW. Barker first gained attention with his YouTube feature  Milk & Serial and is on a similar trajectory to Talk to Me  directors Danny and Michael Philippou; he was just hired to direct A24’s reimagining  of The Texas Chainsaw Massacre. — James

entries.name: In the Grey
entries.description: Jake Gyllenhaal, Eiza González, and Henry Cavill are becoming regular Guy Richie Players. The trio team up in In the Grey to recover a billion dollars from a despot with his own private mercenary army, with González running the show as she bails out Gyllenhaal and Cavill, playing special ops agents with an expected buddy-cop dynamic. This is standard fare from Richie, who has become arguably the busiest action director in Hollywood. While he’s found great success on streaming of late with “Young Sherlock” and “The Gentlemen,” In the Grey’s box office prospects are looking dim, potentially due to the fact the movie is being released by Black Bear, a veteran indie production company that’s just getting its legs as a full-fledged distribution company. In other words: In the Grey will steal billions on streaming. — Arno

entries.name: Is God Is
entries.description: Vivica A. Fox plays a burned and bedridden mother named God in this revenge tale where her daughters, one of them disfigured in a fire when she was little, look to settle up with their father, the man who set their home ablaze years ago. This is the trailer I’ve watched the most this year while avoiding any story/spoiler details. I just want to watch Kara Young and Mallori Johnson, two actresses who are at the top of the call sheet for the first time in their respective careers, hit the road to hunt down Sterling K. Brown. Aleshea Harris adapted her play to make her directorial debut, too. — Arno

entries.name: Jack Ryan: Ghost War
entries.description: Jack Ryan gets the “Peaky Blinders” treatment with a one-off movie set roughly three years after John Krasinski’s series ended. Ghost War finds the former CIA operative back in the fold with pretty much everyone he used to work with (including the dream Wendell Pierce) when a black ops team from his past resurfaces. Krasinski scored a screenplay credit here, too (he wasn’t a writer on the series). Despite Amazon MGM Studios’ deep investment in theatrical releases this year, this movie is debuting on Prime Video on May 20, which makes sense.

entries.name: Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed
entries.detail: Series premieres on Apple TV May 20
entries.description: With all apologies to “She-Hulk,” I’ve been waiting nearly a decade for Tatiana Maslany to return to TV with a series I want to snack on week to  week. In “Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed,” the former “Black  Mirror” star plays an overtaxed single parent (because apparently there  is no other kind of divorced mom), who gets into detective/true-crime mode  after witnessing what she thinks was a crime. The promo materials say the  series is a lighter touch whodunit, but there’s a scene in the trailer with a  power saw that gave me the yikes. Season 1 consists of 10 episodes; the first  two episodes premiere back-to-back on Apple TV on May 20 followed by weekly  drops every Wednesday. — Arno

entries.name: The Boroughs
entries.description: It’s seniors versus spooky creatures in this new series from the creators of “The Dark Crystal: Age of Resistance.” Produced by the Duffer Brothers, this eight-episode series kinda sorta feels like “Stranger Things” for the over 65 crowd. We’re not complaining, especially with such a stellar cast: Bill Pullman, Geena Davis, Alfred Molina, Alfre Woodard, Jena Malone, and Denis O’Hare are just a few of the excellent ensemble. Now that’s a monster hunting crew we’d happily join. — Adrienne

entries.name: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu
entries.description: Although we would have really loved a fourth season of “Awesome Space Dad,” we're happy to get to spend a little more time following the adventures of Din Djarin and Grogu. “The Mandalorian” finale left us with the promise of Djarin using his bounty hunter talents in the service of the New Republic, hunting down the Imperial warlords scattered throughout the galaxy, and that's what Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu looks to deliver. Sigourney Weaver joins the cast as military leader and starfighter pilot Colonel Ward, and keep your ears open for Jeremy Allen White as the voice of warlord Rotta the Hutt and Martin Scorsese as an Ardennian fry cook. — Vanessa

entries.name: I Love Boosters
entries.description: I love Boots Riley. As a filmmaker and a musician. Sorry to Bother You, I recommend it to this day. (“I’m a Virgo” didn’t work for me the first time around, but friends have suggested to give it a second chance.) But I Love Boosters has been at the top of my Watchlist since it was announced Keke Palmer would lead the Velvet Gang, a crew of shoplifters who steal and resell the clothes that belong to a single, specific fashion designer. Knowing Demi Moore plays opposite Keke, is there anything else you need to get you out to the movies? — Arno

entries.name: Spider-Noir
entries.description: Spider-Man is truly headed into the Spider-Verse with this new series starring Nicolas Cage in his first-ever TV leading role. “Spider-Noir” follows down-and-out PI Ben Reilly (Cage) as he is forced to step back into his superhero persona, The Spider, to solve a puzzling case. Spider-fans will remember Cage’s character from his introduction in the animated feature Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse, and we’re looking forward to seeing Cage bring this character in front of the camera. Speaking of cameras, the eight-episode series will be released in black-and white, but there will also be a period-accurate color version too. Does that mean technicolor web slinging? We sure hope so. — Adrienne

entries.name: Backrooms
entries.description: Have you ever been in a place that just felt … off? Writer-director Kane Parsons is ready to take you to the original liminal space with his feature film debut, Backrooms. Inspired by the viral creepypasta and Parsons’ original short films, this A24 horror flick follows a man’s exploration of an impossible set of rooms on the other side of a strange doorway in the basement of a furniture store. Parsons was just 17 when he uploaded his first Backrooms short to YouTube and went viral for his impressive VFX skills and keen directorial eye. Now, just three years later the young artist is releasing a feature horror film through A24. Not too shabby, if you ask me. — Adrienne

entries.name: Pressure
entries.description: It’s been the better part of a decade since we’ve heard from Hotel Mumbai writer-director Anthony Maras. The filmmaker returns with a WWII drama that depicts the meetings between U.S. President Eisenhower and James Stagg, Captain of the UK’s Royal Air Force, right before D-Day in June 1944, the campaign that turned the tide of the war in favor of the Allies. The film offers prestige roles for Brendan Fraser (Eisenhower) and Andrew Scott (Stagg) in what could be the Nuremberg of 2026; meaning, an older adult skewing movie that sticks around in theaters and steadily accrues as impressive box office tally (Nuremberg made nearly $60 million worldwide). — Arno

entries.name: The Breadwinner
entries.description: Nate Barzgate, the most financially successful comedian in the world, brings his relatable style of comedy to the big screen for the first time in The Breadwinner. The movie feels like a modern day update of Mr. Mom if you were an ‘80s latchkey kid. From the trailer, I sense Barzgate’s kind of dad seems most befuddled about how to run a household day to day; he's not pressed when his wife, played by the always welcome Mandy Moore, becomes the title character when her product invention is backed by the “Shark Tank” crew. — Arno

entries.name: Star City
entries.description: “For All Mankind” fans are eating well this year. Not only did they get a fifth season of Ronald D. Moore’s speculative alternate history, but they’re also getting the spin-off series “Star City.” This new show takes viewers back to the space race and behind the Iron Curtain to explore the lives of the cosmonauts, engineers, and intelligence officers responsible for their first moon landing. — Adrienne

plot_text.plain_text: Miranda Priestly navigates her career amid the decline of traditional magazine publishing and reunites with Andy Sachs to face off against a former assistant turned rival.

node.text: Hokum: La maldición de la bruja

plot_text.plain_text: A horror writer visits an Irish inn to scatter his parents' ashes, unaware the property is said to be haunted by a witch.

Relates the tale of a group of British boys who become stuck on a tropical island that is unoccupied and who gradually slide into anarchy as social conventions disappear and attempts to rule responsibly fail.
Supernatural, semihistorical horror anthology series, where each season is inspired by a different infamous or mysterious real-life historical tragedy.

node.text: Mortal Kombat: Cuộc Chiến Sinh Tử II

plot_text.plain_text: The fan favorite champions -- now joined by Johnny Cage himself -- are pitted against one another in the ultimate battle to defeat the dark rule of Shao Kahn that threatens the very existence of the Earthrealm and its defenders.

node.text: Bèèèèh: Het Mysterie van de Wei
Đội Thám Tử Cừu: Án Mạng Lúc Nửa Đêm
Three Bags Full: A Sheep Detective Movie

plot_text.plain_text: Every night a shepherd reads aloud a murder mystery, pretending his sheep can understand. When he is found dead, the sheep realize at once that it was a murder and think they know everything about how to go about solving it.

node.text: A Marvel Television Special Presentation: The Punisher: One Last Kill
Marvel Television przedstawia - Punisher: Ostatnie starcie

plot_text.plain_text: As Frank Castle searches for meaning beyond revenge, an unexpected force pulls him back into the fight.
The End of the World is coming, which means a fussy Angel and a loose-living Demon who've become overly fond of life on Earth are forced to form an unlikely alliance to stop Armageddon.
Docuseries chronicling the purchase and stewardship of Wrexham AFC, one of professional football's oldest clubs, by two Hollywood actors, Ryan Reynolds and Rob McElhenney.

After breaking the mysterious "One Wish Willow" to win his crush's heart, a hopeless romantic finds himself getting exactly what he asked for but soon discovers that some desires come at a dark, sinister price.

A covert team of elite operatives are living in the shadows. When a ruthless despot steals a billion-dollar fortune, they're sent to take it back-an impossible heist that erupts into a deadly game of strategy, deception and survival.
Two sisters embark on an epic quest for revenge; confronting a charged family history that will push them to extraordinary lengths.

plot_text.plain_text: Follows Jack Ryan who reunites with CIA operatives to navigate a treacherous web of betrayal against an enemy who knows their every move, facing a past they thought was long put to rest.
A divorced mom gets caught up in a perilous web of blackmail, murder, and youth soccer.
In a seemingly picturesque retirement community, a group of unlikely heroes must band together to stop an otherworldly threat from stealing the one thing they don't have - time.

plot_text.plain_text: Once a lone bounty hunter, Mandalorian Din Djarin and his apprentice Grogu embark on an exciting new Star Wars adventure.

original_title_text.text: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu

plot_text.plain_text: A group of shoplifters take aim at a cutthroat fashion maven.
An aging and down on his luck private investigator in 1930s New York is forced to grapple with his past life as the city's one and only superhero.
After a therapist's patient disappears into a dimension beyond reality, she must venture into the unknown to save him.

In the tense 72 hours before D-Day, General Dwight D. Eisenhower and Captain James Stagg face an impossible choice--launch the most dangerous seaborne invasion in history or risk losing the war altogether.
A supermom lands a Shark Tank deal, switching roles with her breadwinner husband. He struggles to adapt as a stay-at-home dad to their three daughters.
Soviet cosmonauts, engineers, and intelligence officers risk everything in an ambitious space program aiming to be the first to reach the moon.
